_comment: HAND-BUILT v2 findings fixture (Build 17). Arena scan for the squamous-nsclc indication. FACTS + sources + coverage_notes only; NO read_through/thesis_bearing/priority — the manager's. Facts grounded in docs/schema/sample-issue-hmbd-001-2026-07-18.json and docs/research/program-detective-source-set-2026.md. registry_delta demonstrates the registry-watch input class (spec/04).

findings.summary: Ivonescimab (AK112), Akeso's PD-1 x VEGF bispecific, showed an overall-survival benefit (HR 0.66; 27.9 vs 23.7 months) over PD-1 plus chemotherapy in first-line squamous NSCLC in HARMONi-6, presented at the ASCO 2026 Plenary. The trial was conducted in China only; regional generalizability is a live dispute.

The current first-line standard of care in squamous NSCLC remains pembrolizumab plus platinum/taxane (the KEYNOTE-407 regimen), PD-L1 agnostic. No primary-sourced later-line displacement surfaced this cycle.

proposed_entity.name: Pembrolizumab + platinum/taxane (KEYNOTE-407 regimen)
proposed_entity.what_it_is: Merck & Co.'s first-line squamous-NSCLC standard-of-care chemo-immunotherapy regimen.

findings.summary: Registry watch: no in-window US-registry delta for the tracked HER3-DXd squamous NCT this cycle; the movement surfaced via the ESMO title-drop instead. The tracked HMBD-001 Phase 1b NCT (chemotherapy backbone +/- cetuximab) showed no module change.

coverage_notes.notes: Ivonescimab's HARMONi-6 OS win is the defining first-line squamous event; caveat that the trial was China-only. SOC unchanged this cycle. Registry poll returned no US delta for the HER3-DXd squamous NCT; China-first assets remain the coverage gap for this setting.
